volumeTitle: Birds. Lysistrata. Women at the Thesmophoria
label: Birds (Section 354b - Section 448)

sections.label: Section 354b
sections.translation: Wait ! Stay here !

sections.label: Section 355
sections.translation: That they may tear me to pieces ?

sections.label: Section 355b
sections.translation: And how do you think to escape them ?

sections.label: Section 356b
sections.translation: I don ' t know at all .

sections.label: Section 356c
sections.translation: Come , I will tell you . We must stop and fight them . Let us arm ourselves with these stew-pots .

sections.label: Section 358
sections.original: τί δὲ χύτρα νώ γʼ ὠφελήσει ;
sections.translation: Why with the stew-pots ?

sections.label: Section 358b
sections.translation: The owl will not attack us then .

sections.label: Section 359
sections.translation: But do you see all those hooked claws ?

sections.label: Section 359b
sections.translation: Take the spit

sections.label: Section 360
sections.translation: and pierce the foe on your side .

sections.label: Section 360b
sections.translation: And how about my eyes ?

sections.label: Section 361
sections.original: ὀξύβαφον ἐντευθενὶ προσδοῦ λαβὼν ἢ τρύβλιον .
sections.translation: Protect them with this dish or this vinegar-pot .

sections.label: Section 362
sections.original: ὦ σοφώτατʼ , εὖ γʼ ἀνηῦρες αὐτὸ καὶ στρατηγικῶς ·
sections.translation: Oh ! what cleverness ! what inventive genius ! You are a great general , even greater than Nicias , where stratagem is concerned .

sections.label: Section 364
sections.original: ἐλελελεῦ χώρει κάθες τὸ ῥύγχος · οὐ μέλλειν ἐχρῆν .
sections.translation: Forward , forward , charge with your beaks ! Come , no delay .

sections.label: Section 365
sections.original: ἕλκε τίλλε παῖε δεῖρε , κόπτε πρώτην τὴν χύτραν .
sections.translation: Tear , pluck , strike , flay them , and first of all smash the stew-pot .

sections.label: Section 366
sections.original: εἰπέ μοι τί μέλλετʼ ὦ πάντων κάκιστα θηρίων
sections.translation: Oh , most cruel of all animals , why tear these two men to pieces , why kill them ? What have they done to you ? They belong to the same tribe , to the same family as my wife .

sections.label: Section 369
sections.original: φεισόμεσθα γάρ τι τῶνδε μᾶλλον ἡμεῖς ἢ λύκων ;
sections.translation: Are wolves to be spared ? Are they not our most mortal foes ?

sections.label: Section 370
sections.original: ἢ τίνας τεισαίμεθʼ ἄλλους τῶνδʼ ἂν ἐχθίους ἔτι ;
sections.translation: So let us punish them .

sections.label: Section 371
sections.original: εἰ δὲ τὴν φύσιν μὲν ἐχθροὶ τὸν δὲ νοῦν εἰσιν φίλοι ,
sections.translation: If they are your foes by nature , they are your friends in heart , and they come here to give you useful advice .

sections.label: Section 373
sections.original: πῶς δʼ ἂν οἵδʼ ἡμᾶς τι χρήσιμον διδάξειάν ποτε
sections.translation: Advice or a useful word from their lips , from them , the enemies of my forebears ?

sections.label: Section 375
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἀπʼ ἐχθρῶν δῆτα πολλὰ μανθάνουσιν οἱ σοφοί .
sections.translation: The wise can often profit by the lessons of a foe , for caution is the mother of safety . It is just such a thing as one will not learn from a friend and which an enemy compels you to know . To begin with , it ' s the foe and not the friend that taught cities to build high walls , to equip long vessels of war ;

sections.label: Section 380
sections.original: τὸ δὲ μάθημα τοῦτο σῴζει παῖδας οἶκον χρήματα .
sections.translation: and it ' s this knowledge that protects our children , our slaves and our wealth .

sections.label: Section 381
sections.original: ἔστι μὲν λόγων ἀκοῦσαι πρῶτον , ὡς ἡμῖν δοκεῖ ,
sections.translation: Well then , I agree , let us first hear them , for that is best ; one can even learn something in an enemy ' s school .

sections.label: Section 383
sections.original: οἵδε τῆς ὀργῆς χαλᾶν εἴξασιν . ἄναγʼ ἐπὶ σκέλος .
sections.translation: Their wrath seems to cool . Draw back a little .

sections.label: Section 384
sections.original: καὶ δίκαιόν γʼ ἐστὶ κἀμοὶ δεῖ νέμειν ὑμᾶς χάριν .
sections.translation: It ' s only justice , and you will thank me later .

sections.label: Section 385
sections.original: ἀλλὰ μὴν οὐδʼ ἄλλο σοί πω πρᾶγμʼ ἐνηντιώμεθα .
sections.translation: Never have we opposed your advice up to now .

sections.label: Section 386
sections.original: μᾶλλον εἰρήνην ἄγουσι νὴ Δίʼ , ὥστε τὴν χύτραν
sections.translation: They are in a more peaceful mood ; put down your stew-pot and your two dishes ; spit in hand , doing duty for a spear , let us mount guard inside the camp

sections.label: Section 390
sections.translation: close to the pot and watch in our arsenal closely ; for we must not fly .

sections.label: Section 393
sections.translation: You are right . But where shall we be buried , if we die ?

sections.label: Section 395
sections.translation: In the Ceramicus ; for , to get a public funeral , we shall tell the Strategi that we fell at Orneae , fighting the country ' s foes .

sections.label: Section 400
sections.original: ἄναγʼ ἐς τάξιν πάλιν ἐς ταὐτόν ,
sections.translation: Return to your ranks and lay down your courage beside your wrath as the hoplites do . Then let us ask these men who they are , whence they come ,

sections.label: Section 405
sections.translation: and with what intent . Here , Epops , answer me .

sections.label: Section 407
sections.translation: Are you calling me ? What do you want of me ?

sections.label: Section 408
sections.translation: Who are they ? From what country ?

sections.label: Section 409
sections.translation: Strangers , who have come from Greece , the land of the wise .

sections.label: Section 410
sections.translation: And what fate has led them hither to the land of the birds ?

sections.label: Section 412b
sections.translation: Their love for you and their wish to share your kind of life ; to dwell and

sections.label: Section 415
sections.translation: remain with you always .

sections.label: Section 416
sections.translation: Indeed , and what are their plans ?

sections.label: Section 417
sections.translation: They are wonderful , incredible , unheard of .

sections.label: Section 418
sections.translation: Why , do they think to see some advantage that determines them to settle here ? Are they hoping with our help

sections.label: Section 420
sections.original: κρατεῖν ἂν ἢ τὸν ἐχθρὸν ἢ
sections.translation: to triumph over their foes or to be useful to their friends ?

sections.label: Section 422
sections.translation: They speak of benefits so great it is impossible either to describe or conceive them ; all shall be yours ,

sections.label: Section 425
sections.original: τὸ τῇδε καὶ τὸ κεῖσε καὶ
sections.translation: all that we see here , there , above and below us ; this they vouch for .

sections.label: Section 427
sections.translation: Are they mad ?

sections.label: Section 428
sections.translation: They are the sanest people in the world .

sections.label: Section 429
sections.translation: Clever men ?

sections.label: Section 430
sections.translation: The slyest of foxes , cleverness its very self , men of the world , cunning , the cream of knowing folk .

sections.label: Section 432
sections.translation: Tell them to speak and speak quickly ; why , as I listen to you , I am beside myself with delight .

sections.label: Section 435
sections.original: ἄγε δὴ σὺ καὶ σὺ τὴν πανοπλίαν μὲν πάλιν
sections.translation: Here , you there , take all these weapons and hang them up inside close to the fire , near the figure of the god who presides there and under his protection ; To Pisthetaerus . as for you , address the birds , tell them why I have gathered them together .

sections.label: Section 439b
sections.original: μὰ τὸν Ἀπόλλω ʼγὼ μὲν οὔ ,
sections.translation: Not I , by Apollo ,

sections.label: Section 440
sections.original: ἢν μὴ διάθωνταί γʼ οἵδε διαθήκην ἐμοὶ
sections.translation: unless they agree with me as the little ape of an armorer agreed with his wife , not to bite me , nor pull me by the balls , nor shove things into my . . .

sections.label: Section 442b
sections.translation: Do you mean this ?

sections.label: Section 443b
sections.translation: No , I mean my eyes .

sections.label: Section 444
sections.translation: Agreed .

sections.label: Section 444b
sections.translation: Swear it .

sections.label: Section 445
sections.original: ὄμνυμʼ ἐπὶ τούτοις , πᾶσι νικᾶν τοῖς κριταῖς
sections.translation: I swear it and , if I keep my promise , let judges and spectators give me the victory unanimously .

sections.label: Section 446b
sections.translation: It is a bargain .

sections.label: Section 447
sections.original: εἰ δὲ παραβαίην , ἑνὶ κριτῇ νικᾶν μόνον .
sections.translation: And if I break my word , may I succeed by one vote only .

sections.label: Section 448
sections.translation: Hearken , ye people ! Hoplites , pick up your weapons and return to your firesides ;
